original_hypothesis.title: BOLDUpgradeAction DoS via inboxMaxCount == 0
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: `StateHashPreImageLookup.get()` uses `inboxMaxCount != 0` to check if a hash exists. If the legitimate state has `inboxMaxCount == 0`, the upgrade will revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Sentinel value collisions are a common source of edge-case bugs. If the genesis state or a very early state is being upgraded, `inboxMaxCount` might legitimately be 0.
preconditions: The latest confirmed assertion being upgraded has inboxMaxCount == 0

initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No separate boolean flag for existence

analysis_questions: Can a valid assertion have inboxMaxCount == 0?
Will this block the BOLD upgrade?
validation_requirements: Attempt to execute the BOLD upgrade

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: The protocol attempts to upgrade to BOLD while the rollup is in its genesis state (inboxMaxCount == 0). The upgrade transaction reverts, preventing the deployment of the new challenge manager.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Delay or failure of protocol upgrade
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires creating a new assertion to advance inboxMaxCount > 0 before upgrading

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for upgrades from genesis state

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
